mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points within a single month signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off selling and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.4pp, or the signal loses statistical significance in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which drives corporate earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns, with a combined regression coefficient statistically indistinguishable from zero over 5 years.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side constraints and falling capacity utilization, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO contractions during tightening periods yield positive 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases, or the predictive alpha decays to zero within 12 months.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL declines while FEDFUNDS remains stable or falls, real borrowing costs decrease, boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS shows no predictive power for 5-day returns, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.01 across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month declines in total nonfarm payrolls indicate weakening labor demand and reduced household income, which suppresses corporate revenue growth and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS declines fail to predict negative 60-day SPX returns, with a rolling 3-year correlation coefficient consistently above -0.05 or statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP growth rate exceeding 2.5 percent signals robust aggregate demand that outpaces corporate cost inflation, driving earnings upgrades and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are not significantly higher following GDPC1 prints above 2.5%, or the signal generates negative alpha in > 50% of post-2010 observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production during a high-volatility regime reflects deteriorating manufacturing output and supply bottlenecks, which compresses profit margins and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during high-volatility periods fail to predict negative 5-day SPX returns, with a hit rate below 40% over a 5-year walk-forward evaluation.
